deck_goal: Help Chleo Lee evaluate and pitch a practical Singapore community band room without signing a risky lease too early.

researcher.summary: Market pricing, legal/licensing, property and cost claims are source-backed or labeled as planning models.
evidence_gaps: Final cost and legality still require a real address, landlord answers, contractor quotes and any required QP review.

story_strategist.summary: The deck now follows a clean decision path: validate demand, screen the site, quote the build, then decide whether to lease.
top_risks: The proposal is practical, but Chleo still needs live outreach and real site quotes before the plan becomes executable.

designer.summary: The deck has a distinctive light Creative Mode style and the current Playwright pass shows no footer/control overlap, including slide 8, slide 16 and mobile controls.
top_risks: Appendix slides remain dense by design and should be used for Q&A rather than live narration.

critic.summary: The deck is much stronger after repairing stale edit storage and protected chrome. It is ready for human review, not a substitute for professional lease/legal advice.
top_risks: A viewer may still underestimate how site-specific noise and approval risk are.

hard_gates.evidence: validate-claim-ledger and lint-claim-refs pass for examples/chleo-community-band-room.
Legal and property slides defer public showcases and require written approvals, landlord consent and site-specific checks.

Playwright rendered all 22 slides at desktop size and checked high-risk slides at short-laptop and mobile sizes with no overlap issues. The latest pass also verified slide 8, slide 16 and fullscreen controls.
Corrupt legacy edit storage was seeded in browser QA and removed without altering slide 15 text.
User-reported slide failures were reproduced conceptually, repaired and reviewed via contact-sheet screenshots.

dimension_scores.rationale: The deck is focused on Chleo's first decision rather than abstract studio ambition.
dimension_scores.evidence: Slides 2, 6, 7, 8, 14 and 19 give concrete gates before signing.

dimension_scores.rationale: The slide order moves from recommendation to market, demand, site, legal, fitout, gear, money and decision gate.
dimension_scores.evidence: Title-only flow remains coherent across 22 slides.

dimension_scores.rationale: Key factual claims are source-backed; cost and pricing slides are clearly modeled.
dimension_scores.evidence: Claim ledger and claim-reference validators pass.

dimension_scores.rationale: The deck uses tables, cards, charts, a Gantt and gates for distinct jobs, though some appendix slides remain dense.
dimension_scores.evidence: Slides 5, 8, 10, 15, 16, 18, 20 and 21 carry comparison or planning visuals.

dimension_scores.rationale: The visual style is distinctive and the latest browser pass fixed the major instability from stale edits and controls overlap.
dimension_scores.evidence: Desktop, short-laptop and mobile Playwright checks passed.

dimension_scores.rationale: The presenter can explain the decision logic and use appendices for deeper Q&A.
dimension_scores.evidence: Slides 7, 14, 18, 19 and appendices turn the plan into actions.

dimension_scores.rationale: The deck surfaces demand, legal, noise, fitout, cash and lease risks without pretending they are already solved.
dimension_scores.evidence: Slides 8, 10, 11, 12, 14, 15 and 19 name the main blind spots.

strengths: Clear title and memorable proposal framing.
Strong recommendation and decision discipline.
Clear launch boundary for the first product.
Market wedge is visible and grounded in price signals.
Useful competitor table with current price anchors.
Defines discovery targets and what counts as a real lead.
Validation ladder avoids blind pre-sales.
Property scouting steps are concrete and now visually balanced.
Site options are compared with a clear stance.
Use/licensing risk is explained without overclaiming.
Acoustic distinction is simple and important.
Fitout layers show where cost jumps.
Starter gear categories are actionable.
Quote calls are split by vendor type.
Startup ranges are scaled correctly and readable.
Break-even logic is clearer after title and pricing-card repair.
Operating culture is practical and not corny.
Gantt chart turns the plan into weeks and outputs.
Close gives a clear go/no-go gate.
Gear appendix answers model-level questions.
Call-sheet appendix makes vendor outreach concrete.
Cost appendix explains what sits inside the startup ranges.

summary.score_rationale: The deck passes as ready for human review after fixing the local edit corruption bug, protected chrome spacing, title scale on dense slides and all reported footer/control overlap issues.
top_repairs: Use appendices 20 and 21 only for Q&A, not live narration.
Replace planning estimates with vendor quotes once Chleo has a candidate site.
